name: Flush Pearl and Amber Resin Inlay on Satin Rift-Sawn Walnut

source.description: Seed example used to test VOC v0.1 material, surface, depth, pattern, prompt, code, QA, and decision logic.

parameters.name: material_family

compiler_mappings.css_svg: directional grain turbulence and warm brown base layer
compiler_mappings.webgl: low metalness, satin roughness, subtle normal map

parameters.qa_rule: Wood grain direction must remain coherent unless burl or end-grain is specified.

parameters.name: grain_directionality
parameters.definition: Degree to which surface features flow in a single dominant direction.
evidence: Rift-sawn walnut implies strong linear grain.

compiler_mappings.css_svg: use directional turbulence stretched along Y axis

parameters.qa_rule: Reject random grain unless a mutation explicitly changes the cut or figure.

parameters.name: wood_displacement_strength
evidence: Satin finished walnut should show subtle relief, not heavy carving.
parameters.qa_rule: Do not exaggerate grain into carved channels unless explicitly transformed.

parameters.name: depth_level_resin_fill
parameters.definition: Depth position of the resin fill relative to the walnut base plane.
evidence: Flush inlay means fill rises to the base plane.

compiler_mappings.css: avoid drop shadow; use edge-darkening and specular overlay

parameters.qa_rule: Flush inlay should not cast the same shadow as an applied ornament.

parameters.name: pearl_iridescence
parameters.definition: Subtle structural color effect from mother-of-pearl fragments.

compiler_mappings.prompt: subtle iridescent mother-of-pearl fragments suspended beneath clear resin

parameters.qa_rule: Avoid rainbow overload unless experimental mode is active.

parameters.name: complexity
parameters.definition: Overall visual density and cognitive load.
evidence: Wood grain, resin, pearl, carved channels, and optional brass create richness but remain bounded.

relationships.compiler_effect: Provides base color, grain, roughness, displacement, and prompt language.

evidence: Resin fill is described as flush.
relationships.qa_effect: Requires edge-darkening instead of raised drop shadow.

evidence: Example pattern supports radial channels, though final layout remains adjustable.
relationships.constraint_effect: Mutation may switch radial to asymmetrical marquetry only if pattern QA remains above threshold.

avoid: neon acrylic base in production mode
hard_constraints: Flush resin fill must remain at base-plane level unless transformed into applied ornament.
Gold or pearl particles must remain inside the resin or channel boundary.
exception_rules: High iridescence is allowed in wild-lab mode only.

transformations.reason: Create a reusable image-generation prompt from canonical material, surface, depth, and pattern values.

notes: Use process language such as inlay-inspired or marquetry-inspired unless a specific tradition is documented.
SVG implementation should keep displacement subtle for performance.

decision_log.context: How to store rendering values for material surfaces.
decision_log.chosen_path: Canonical surface profile first, renderer-specific mappings second.
decision_log.why_chosen: Preserves interoperability across prompt, SVG, CSS, WebGL, Figma, and future targets.
decision_log.why_not_alternative_1_now: Too renderer-specific for the root ontology.
decision_log.why_not_alternative_2_now: Useful but not precise enough for code and QA.
decision_log.risk: Requires compiler mappings before visual output is automatic.
decision_log.revisit_condition: If the first product export is SVG-only.
decision_log.future_use: Create an SVG-first lightweight asset pack pathway.
